steps.title: Recognize the radical as fractional exponent
steps.content:
The expression $\sqrt[4]{x^7}$ represents the fourth root of $x^7$. By the definition of fractional exponents, the index of the radical becomes the denominator of the exponent, and the power inside becomes the numerator:
$$\sqrt[4]{x^7} = x^{7/4}$$

steps.title: Match the exponent form to find m
steps.content:
Since the expression is rewritten as $x^m$ in exponential form, we equate:
$$x^m = x^{7/4}$$
When two powers with the same base are equal, their exponents must be equal. Therefore $m = \frac{7}{4}$ or $1.75$. The answer is 7/4.
